medical_history.chief_complaint: Hypotonia, significant delay in motor milestones, and marked muscle weakness since birth with mild improvement over time
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Mechanically ventilated since birth and G-tube fed. Tolerated being off the ventilator for up to 8 hours during the day. Speech was delayed but able to repeat words and communicate with his parents. Previous evaluations included two muscle biopsies; one was interpreted as displaying type 2 fiber atrophy and the other to show neurogenic atrophy. The patient had a mild generalized aminoaciduria.

physical_examination.general_examination: Cup-shaped low-set ears and contractures at the metacarpophalangeal and proximal interphalangeal joints involving most fingers. Bifacial diplegia, high arched palate, but good head control, and could sit with minimal support. The tendon reflexes were hypoactive.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: EMG showed prominent fibrillation potentials and rapidly recruiting small motor unit potentials with occasional complexity.
